apiOrSchemaChanges: CertificationRecord gains field `Unsupported bool` (default false, backward-compatible)
failingTest: (none yet — the field doesn't exist, so no test can reference it; tests will be added as part of this work)
fileMap: internal/domain/record.go — modify — add `Unsupported bool` field to CertificationRecord struct
internal/report/detailed.go — modify — skip Unsupported records in computeDimensionAverages and computeLanguageBreakdowns
internal/report/full.go — modify — skip Unsupported records in buildLanguageDetail
internal/report/health.go — modify — skip Unsupported records in Health() with adjusted denominator
internal/report/card.go — modify — skip Unsupported records in GenerateCard() with adjusted TotalUnits
internal/report/detailed_test.go — modify — add subtests verifying Unsupported records are excluded from dimension/language aggregates
internal/report/full_test.go — modify — add subtests verifying Unsupported records are excluded from per-language aggregates
internal/report/health_test.go — modify — add subtests verifying Unsupported records are excluded from pass-rate/score
internal/report/card_test.go — modify — add subtests verifying Unsupported records are excluded from card aggregates
fixApproach: Add `Unsupported bool` to `domain.CertificationRecord` (default `false`, backward-compatible), then guard each of the four report-aggregation loops — `computeDimensionAverages`, `buildLanguageDetail`, `Health`, `GenerateCard` (and the local `computeLanguageBreakdowns` in detailed.go) — with `if r.Unsupported { continue }`. Adjust denominator counters (`TotalUnits`, `counts`, `accum` entry totals) so unsupported records are excluded from averages, pass rates, and grade distributions. Add table-driven subtests to existing test files that insert one or more `Unsupported: true` records and assert they contribute nothing to aggregates. Existing tests continue to pass because the new field defaults to `false`.
openQuestions: The Unsupported field does not exist on CertificationRecord yet. The non-goal says "Do not modify domain types," but this work requires adding the field — is adding Unsupported to CertificationRecord acceptable, or should we wait for parent issue #25 to land first?
Should Unsupported records still be counted in TotalUnits for display purposes (even if excluded from averages), or fully excluded from all counts?
Should the Unsupported field be serialized/deserialized in JSON (evidence output), or is it a runtime-only flag?
steps: Add `Unsupported bool` field to `domain.CertificationRecord` struct in `internal/domain/record.go` (defaults to false, backward-compatible)
In `internal/report/detailed.go`: guard `computeDimensionAverages` loop with `if r.Unsupported { continue }`; apply same guard in the local `computeLanguageBreakdowns` function
In `internal/report/full.go`: guard `buildLanguageDetail` loop with `if r.Unsupported { continue }` so unsupported records are excluded from per-language score/grades/passing aggregates

In `internal/report/health.go`: guard `Health()` loop with `if r.Unsupported { continue }`; use a separate counted-total for denominators instead of `len(records)` so PassRate and AverageScore exclude unsupported records

In `internal/report/card.go`: guard `GenerateCard()` loop with `if r.Unsupported { continue }`; compute `TotalUnits` as the counted (non-unsupported) total so OverallScore, PassRate, and GradeDistribution exclude unsupported records

Add subtests to `internal/report/card_test.go`, `internal/report/health_test.go`, `internal/report/full_test.go`, and `internal/report/detailed_test.go` that insert `Unsupported: true` records and assert zero contribution to aggregates
Run `go test ./... -count=1` and confirm all existing and new tests pass
summary: Add Unsupported field to CertificationRecord and skip unsupported records in report aggregate calculations across four report consumers
